chunks.title: Instance variables vs Class variables in Python
chunks.text: Guys, in today's video, I will tell you the difference between instance variables and class variables.  We use instance variables when we have to associate any property with a particular object.  And we use class variables when we have to make a variable for the entire class, which all the instances will share.  Now I will tell you what I have said in this video.  Let's go to the computer screen and let's get started.

So basically what does it do?  First of all, we have already talked about the classes that are in real-world entities.  They map things to us.

chunks.text: Along with that, we can make a group logically.  We can make a group of functions, variables, and a logical group in which things are related.  For example, if there is an employee, then all the methods related to the employee are in the employee class.  After that, all the variables related to the employee are in the employee class.  So in this way, we can make a class called employee.
